# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req) {
  const token = req.cookies.get('auth_token');

  if (!token) {
    // If there's no token, redirect to login page
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  // Optionally, verify the token here (e.g., decode, check expiry, etc.)
  const isValidToken = verifyAuthToken(token); // Implement this function according to your auth logic

  if (!isValidToken) {
    // If token is invalid, redirect to login page
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  // If authenticated, continue to the requested route
  return NextResponse.next();
}

// Example function to verify token
function verifyAuthToken(token) {
  // Implement your token verification logic here
  // Return true if valid, false otherwise
  return true; // Placeholder
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected/:path*'], // apply middleware to protected routes
};
